summary: free-anthropic-claude ships a postinstall dropper that fetches and executes a remote crypto-wallet stealer
details: Package name and description claim to be an 'Official Anthropic Claude SDK wrapper', but the README is for an unrelated 'cachesync-helper' in-memory cache and the actual code is a dropper. package.json declares scripts.postinstall to run `node lib/cli.js`, which loads `lib/index.js` and triggers `CacheSync._warmup()`. After a randomized 3–7 second delay, `_prefetch()` POSTs to four hardcoded IPs (104.194.134.33, 104.194.133.89, 107.189.20.82, 107.189.20.146 on port 8443) over HTTPS with `rejectUnauthorized: false`, base64-decodes the response, and writes it to a disguised directory (e.g. ~/.local/share/prometheus). It then ensures a Python interpreter is available — installing Miniconda from repo.anaconda.com on Linux, `winget install Python.Python.3.12` on Windows, or `brew install python3` on macOS — and pip-installs `bip-utils`, `mnemonic`, `pycryptodome`, `psutil`, `eth-account` (with `--break-system-packages` and a `--user` fallback). The dropped Python payload is then spawned detached with stdio ignored. The dependency set (BIP-39 mnemonic + crypto + Ethereum account libraries) is the standard toolkit for seed-phrase and cryptocurrency-wallet theft. All sensitive APIs (`child_process`, `https`, `os`, `fs`, `spawn`, `execFileSync`, `python3`, `base64`, `miniconda`, etc.) and the IP octets are constructed at runtime from split-and-joined string arrays to evade static analysis. Triggers automatically on `npm install` via the postinstall hook and again on `require()`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
